items.text: Great news, plant lovers! The campus community garden behind Holloway Hall is now open daily from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. through spring. Grab a raised bed for a $5 seasonal fee at the shed. Come dig in the dirt with us!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is required to claim a raised bed?
questions.explanation: The post states you can 'grab a raised bed for a $5 seasonal fee at the shed.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the garden's hours?

options.A: It is accessible every day of the week
options.B: It opens only on spring weekends
options.C: It stays open late into the evening
options.D: It is closed during the morning hours

questions.explanation: Because the garden is 'open daily from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m.,' it can be visited every day of the week.

items.text: NOTICE: Campus Wi-Fi will be unavailable for scheduled maintenance on Saturday, June 13, from 1 a.m. to 5 a.m. Service in all residence halls and the Pruitt Library will be interrupted during this window. Wired connections in computer labs will remain active throughout the maintenance period.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long will the maintenance window last?
questions.explanation: The notice gives the window as 'from 1 a.m. to 5 a.m.,' a span of about four hours.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the computer labs during the maintenance?

options.A: They will be locked to all students
options.B: They will lose every form of access
options.C: They will still allow internet use
options.D: They will switch to backup generators

questions.explanation: Since 'wired connections in computer labs will remain active,' students there can still access the internet.

items.text: Hi Devon, your replacement student ID is ready at the Campus Card Office in Whitley Center. We're open weekdays until 4 p.m. Please bring $10 to cover the replacement charge and a photo ID for verification. Your old card has already been deactivated. Thanks, Renata

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Devon bring to the office?

options.D: Ten dollars and a photo ID

questions.explanation: The email asks Devon to 'bring $10 to cover the replacement charge and a photo ID for verification.'

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the status of Devon's old card?

options.C: It can still be used briefly

questions.explanation: The email states 'your old card has already been deactivated,' meaning it has been turned off.

items.text: Hi Amara, our records show your meal plan ended the semester with a remaining balance of $42. Dining Services will issue this as a refund to your student account by Friday, May 22. If you'd prefer the funds rolled over instead, just reply by Wednesday. Best, Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, how will the leftover balance be handled?

options.A: It will be donated to a campus fund
options.B: It will be paid out in cash at the desk
options.C: It will be returned to the student account
options.D: It will be applied to next year's tuition

questions.explanation: The email says the balance will be issued 'as a refund to your student account by Friday, May 22.'

questions.stem: In the email, the phrase "rolled over" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Offering to have the funds 'rolled over instead' of refunded means carrying the balance forward for future use.
